flags.concern: Shot creation cache hydration: the proposed RPC return shape only includes shot_generation ids/timeline frames, but current multi-generation callers only pass generation ids and no per-image preview payload. `CreateShotOptions` has a singular `generationPreview` field only, while the multi-generation callers invoke `createShot({ generationIds })` directly. Unless the RPC itself returns joined generation/primary-variant fields, the client still cannot guarantee immediate thumbnails for the new shot and can fall back to the same empty-then-refetch behavior the plan is trying to remove.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCreationTypes.ts:12-21` defines only a single-item `generationPreview`; `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:246` and `src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/components/GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx:109` both call `createShot({ generationIds })` with ids only.

flags.concern: Shot creation return contract: the plan talks about cache insertion, but it does not make preserving `ShotCreationResult.shot` explicit for the multi-generation path. Existing timeline callers immediately depend on `result.shot` being present; if the executor returns only `shotId` and relies on cache updates, the timeline context menu flow stops returning a created shot and its follow-up actions regress.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:246-252` only clears selection and returns when `result.shot` exists, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:93-100` stores that returned shot for post-create UI.

flags.concern: Shot creation edge flow: the plan's repo read on agent usage is wrong. The timeline agent does not call `createShotWithGenerationsPath`; it has its own service-role helper that still does `insert_shot_at_position` followed by a separate `shot_generations` insert and requires an explicit `position`. If the new RPC is meant to be the root implementation, the plan needs to move or intentionally exclude this caller; otherwise multi-generation shot creation remains split across two implementations with different behavior.
flags.evidence: `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/clips.ts:52-72` implements a separate `createShotWithGenerations`, and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts:97-103` calls it with `position: APPEND_SHOT_POSITION`.
